---
title: "CISP 360 \n Structured Programming"
subtitle: "Introduction to C++"
author: "Dr. Caleb Fowler"
version: "1.0.0"

format:
  revealjs:
    margin: 0.05
    css: ../styles/styles.css
    theme: serif
    slide-number: true
    navigation-mode: vertical
    transition: slide
    incremental: false
    scrollable: false
    controls: true
    hash: true
    history: true
    title-slide: false
    footer: "CISP 360 · Fowler"
    keyboard: true
    include-after-body:
      - text: |
          <script>
          window.Reveal.on('ready', event => {
            Reveal.configure({
              keyboard: {
                34: 'down',
                33: 'up',
                116: 'right',
                190: 'left',
                79: null
              }
            });
          });
          </script>

engine: jupyter
execute:
  eval: true
  echo: true

resources:
 - topics/**/images/**

---

## Last Time

::: {.incremental}
1. **While Loop**
2. **Do-While Loop**
3. **For Loop**
:::


## Today's Agenda

- Input Validation — why and what
- Validation architectures
- Inline and loop-until-valid design patterns
- Validation with `while` and `do-while`
- Increment & decrement operators
- Breaking out of a loop

::: notes

:::



{{< include topics/topic_1_input_validation_why/topic_1_input_validation_why.qmd >}}
{{< include topics/topic_2_input_validation_what/topic_2_input_validation_what.qmd >}}
{{< include topics/topic_3_input_validation_architectures/topic_3_input_validation_architectures.qmd >}}
{{< include topics/topic_4_inline_validation/topic_4_inline_validation.qmd >}}
{{< include topics/topic_5_loop_until_valid/topic_5_loop_until_valid.qmd >}}
{{< include topics/topic_8_whats_the_output_1/topic_8_whats_the_output_1.qmd >}}
{{< include topics/topic_9_increment_decrement/topic_9_increment_decrement.qmd >}}
{{< include topics/topic_10_break_loop/topic_10_break_loop.qmd >}}
{{< include topics/topic_11_whats_the_output_2/topic_11_whats_the_output_2.qmd >}}


# Key Takeaways

- Validate all input — assume nothing.
- Match the architecture to the requirement: inline for one-shot checks, loop-until-valid for re-prompting.
- `while` requires a priming read; `do-while` does not.
- `++` and `--` behave differently in prefix vs. postfix position.
- `break` exits immediately — use it deliberately.


# Next Time
